CROSS-DEFENDANT HAS SHOWN THAT CAUSES OF ACTION 1 AND 2 ARISE AT LEAST IN PART FROM PROTECTED PRIVILEGED CONDUCT IN PARTICULAR THE CLAIM FOR HABITABILITY ISSUES PREPARATORY TO LITIGATION. CROSS-COMPLAINANT HAS NOT SHOWN A LIKELIHOOD OF PREVAILING AS TO THE NON-PRIVILEGED CONDUCT BECAUSE THE ALLEGED MISREPRESENTATION ABOUT VACATING THE PROPERTY ARE NOT STATEMENTS OF FACT ON WHICH ONE COULD REASONABLY RELY, BUT ONLY STATEMENTS OF AN OFFER OR POSSIBILITY. THERE IS NO EVIDENCE OF ANY REPRESENTATION TO CROSS-COMPLAINANT CONCERNING DOYLE'S TENANCY. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J.
